target.title: feat: add Computer Use plugin manifest contract
target.body:
Closes #97938

## What Problem This Solves

Computer Use integrations such as CUA need a static plugin contract OpenClaw can inspect before loading plugin runtime. Without that manifest surface, the macOS app has no cheap way to discover which external plugin owns a Computer Use provider that could bind to OpenClaw.app-owned Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a manifest-first Computer Use contract, `contracts.computerUseProviders`, plus cheap `computerUseProviderMetadata` for macOS host-binding facts such as platform, host protocol, permissions, and MCP server name. The change intentionally does not add a universal Computer Use action schema or runtime registration API; provider plugins still own their MCP/tool mapping and driver behavior.

AI-assisted.

## User Impact

Plugin authors can now declare Computer Use provider ownership in `openclaw.plugin.json`, and future macOS host work can discover those providers without importing every plugin runtime. Existing plugins and runtime behavior are unchanged unless they opt into the new manifest fields.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader-records.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts src/plugins/status.registry-snapshot.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm format:check -- src/plugins/manifest.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.ts src/plugins/registry-types.ts src/plugins/loader-records.ts src/plugins/status-snapshot.ts src/plugins/status.test-helpers.ts src/plugins/loader-records.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts docs/plugins/manifest.md docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

Also attempted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/status.registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/status.test.ts`; `status.registry-snapshot.test.ts` passed, while `status.test.ts` failed before this change's assertions on an existing mock issue: `./plugin-metadata-snapshot.js` does not export `isPluginMetadataSnapshotCompatible` in that test mock.
